It is also referred to as Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ.
== Overview == The region surrounding the {{ill|YЕҚrЕҚ River|ja|йӨҠиҖҒе·қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/YЕҚrЕҚ River|qq=}} basin features archaeological sites such as the {{ill|GЕҚdo Kofun Cluster|lt=GЕҚdo Kofun Cluster|lt=Kamado Kofun Cluster|ja|зҘһй–ҖеҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamado Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, believed to be early 3rd-century keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚen-fun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}),<ref>This interpretation as kofun shifts the beginning of the Kofun period significantly earlier. Others suggest these are late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|ејҘз”ҹжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi period|qq=}} mound burials.</ref> and the {{ill|Inaridai No.1 Kofun|ja|зЁІиҚ·еҸ°1еҸ·еҸӨеўі|Inaridai No.1 Kofun=Inaridai No.1 Kofun|lt=Inaritai No.1 Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaridai No.1 Kofun|qq=}}, which yielded an iron sword inscribed with the royal title {{ill|ЕҢkimi|ja|жІ»еӨ©дёӢеӨ§зҺӢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢkimi|qq=}}.<ref>While some scholars attribute the title to the Yamato court, others link it to the chief buried in the nearby {{ill|Anegasaki Twin Mounds|ja|е§үеҙҺдәҢеӯҗеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Twin Mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=}}.</ref> These indicate close ties with the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Court|qq=}}. In addition, the "{{ill|ManyЕҚshЕ«|ja|дёҮи‘үйӣҶ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ManyЕҚshЕ«|qq=}}" (Volume 14) includes an Eastern song that begins with "Natsuma pulls at the seacoast of KaijЕҚ Lagoon, the boat lingers on the shore as night deepens," suggesting its importance as a hub for waterway traffic.
The placement of {{ill|Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|жӯҰзӨҫеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} territory between Kamitsu Unakami and {{ill|Shimotsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|дёӢжө·дёҠеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} regions implies that the establishment of KokuzЕҚ governance in the BЕҚsЕҚ Peninsula occurred in stages. By the 5th century, the Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ and Shimotsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ collectively exerted influence from central Chiba through parts of modern {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|иҢЁеҹҺзңҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|еҹјзҺүзңҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tokyo|ja|жқұдә¬йғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, a domain sometimes called "Greater Unakami Kuni."
In the 6th century, emerging central forces established Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko, dividing Kamitsu and Shimotsu Unakami regions. Further decline followed the rise of the {{ill|Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|иҸҠйә»еӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} along the {{ill|Murata River|ja|жқ‘з”°е·қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murata River|qq=}}. The {{ill|Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|ja|е§үеҙҺеҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} is considered the burial site of the Kamitsu Unakami lineage, featuring notable tumuli such as the 130-meter-long {{ill|Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|е§үеҙҺеӨ©зҘһеұұеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (late 4th century), the 86-meter-long Shakazan Kofun (early 5th century), and the 103-meter-long Anegasaki Twin Mounds (late 5th century). Shrinking mound sizes in subsequent centuries reflect the lineage's declining power.
=== Lineage === According to the "{{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}," {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|еӨ©з©Ӯж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi|qq=}}вҖҷs son, {{ill|Takehiratori no Mikoto|ja|е»әжҜ”иүҜйіҘе‘Ҫ|1=Takehi-Nateru|1=Takehi-Nateru|zh|е»әжҜ”иүҜйіҘе‘Ҫ|1=Takehi-Nateru|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehiratori no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}},<ref>Takehiratori no Mikoto is also called жӯҰеӨ·йіҘе‘Ҫ, еӨ©еӨ·йіҘе‘Ҫ, or еӨ©ж—Ҙз…§е‘Ҫ in other sources.</ref> was the progenitor of Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ. The "{{ill|KokuzЕҚ Hongi|ja|еӣҪйҖ жң¬зҙҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KokuzЕҚ Hongi|qq=}}" states that during {{ill|Seimu|ja|жҲҗеӢҷеӨ©зҡҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimu|qq=}}вҖҷs reign, Ame-no-Hohi's eighth-generation descendant, {{ill|Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|ja|еҝҚз«ӢеҢ–еӨҡжҜ”е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|qq=}}, was appointed as KokuzЕҚ. His grandson {{ill|Hisatsuki no Atai|ja|д№…йғҪдјҺзӣҙ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisatsuki no Atai|qq=}} later became the Shimotsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ under Emperor {{ill|ЕҢjin|ja|еҝңзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢjin|qq=}}. The Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ lineage, linked to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|еҮәйӣІж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=}}, shared ancestry with other KokuzЕҚ, such as the {{ill|Izumo Province KokuzЕҚ|ja|Izumo Province йҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province KokuzЕҚ|qq=}} and {{ill|Musashi Province KokuzЕҚ|ja|жӯҰи”өеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Province KokuzЕҚ|qq=}}.
The "{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|з¶ҡж—Ҙжң¬зҙҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}}" (Shoku Nihongi) mentions that in 767 ({{ill|Jingo Keiun|ja|зҘһиӯ·жҷҜйӣІ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Keiun|qq=}} 1), {{ill|Himuro Tokunaga|ja|жӘңеүҚиҲҺдәәе»әйә»е‘Ӯ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Tokunaga|qq=}} of KaijЕҚ District was granted the surname "Kazusa Sukune" ({{ill|е®ҝзҰ°|ja|е®ҝзҰ°|1=Sukune|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/е®ҝзҰ°|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukune|qq=}}). Another entry in 775 ({{ill|HЕҚki|ja|е®қдәҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚki|qq=}} 6) notes that he was appointed to an official post in Satsuma, suggesting that he may have been a descendant of the Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|дёҠз·Ҹж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|qq=}}.
The {{ill|Anesaki Shrine|ja|е§үеҹјзҘһзӨҫ|sv|Anesaki-jinja|sv=Anesaki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Shrine|qq=}} is said to have been established by Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto to enshrine the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} and three protective deities. His grandson Oshikane no Mikoto later added {{ill|Nintoku|ja|д»ҒеҫіеӨ©зҡҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nintoku|qq=}} to the enshrined deities. The Anegasaki Kofun Cluster is believed to include burial sites for this KokuzЕҚ lineage, which worshiped Ane Saki Shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|ж°ҸзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|qq=}}.

revision.text:
'''Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko''' (дёҠжө·дёҠеӣҪйҖ , also known as Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ) governed the {{ill|KaijЕҚ District, Kazusa Province|ja|жө·дёҠйғЎ (дёҠз·ҸеӣҪ)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KaijЕҚ District, Kazusa Province|qq=}} in {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|дёҠз·ҸеӣҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa Province|qq=}}, which corresponds to part of present-day {{ill|Ichihara|ja|еёӮеҺҹеёӮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichihara|qq=}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|еҚғи‘үзңҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefecture|qq=}}. It is also referred to as Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ.
== Overview == The region surrounding the {{ill|YЕҚrЕҚ River|ja|йӨҠиҖҒе·қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/YЕҚrЕҚ River|qq=}} basin features archaeological sites such as the {{ill|GЕҚdo Kofun Cluster|lt=GЕҚdo Kofun Cluster|lt=Kamado Kofun Cluster|ja|зҘһй–ҖеҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamado Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, believed to be early 3rd-century keyhole-shaped tombs ({{ill|ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚen-fun|ja|еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=еүҚж–№еҫҢеҶҶеўі|4=ko|5=м „л°©нӣ„мӣҗл¶„|6=zh|7=еүҚж–№еҫҢең“еўі|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ZenpЕҚ-kЕҚen-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}}),<ref>This interpretation as kofun shifts the beginning of the Kofun period significantly earlier. Others suggest these are late {{ill|Yayoi period|ja|ејҘз”ҹжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yayoi period|qq=}} mound burials.</ref> and the {{ill|Inaridai No.1 Kofun|ja|зЁІиҚ·еҸ°1еҸ·еҸӨеўі|Inaridai No.1 Kofun=Inaridai No.1 Kofun|lt=Inaritai No.1 Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaridai No.1 Kofun|qq=}}, which yielded an iron sword inscribed with the royal title {{ill|ЕҢkimi|ja|жІ»еӨ©дёӢеӨ§зҺӢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢkimi|qq=}}.<ref>While some scholars attribute the title to the Yamato court, others link it to the chief buried in the nearby {{ill|Anegasaki Twin Mounds|ja|е§үеҙҺдәҢеӯҗеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Twin Mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=}}.</ref> These indicate close ties with the {{ill|Yamato Court|ja|гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Court|qq=}}. In addition, the "{{ill|ManyЕҚshЕ«|ja|дёҮи‘үйӣҶ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ManyЕҚshЕ«|qq=}}" (Volume 14) includes an Eastern song that begins with "Natsuma pulls at the seacoast of KaijЕҚ Lagoon, the boat lingers on the shore as night deepens," suggesting its importance as a hub for waterway traffic.
The placement of {{ill|Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|жӯҰзӨҫеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} territory between Kamitsu Unakami and {{ill|Shimotsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|дёӢжө·дёҠеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} regions implies that the establishment of KokuzЕҚ governance in the BЕҚsЕҚ Peninsula occurred in stages. By the 5th century, the Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ and Shimotsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ collectively exerted influence from central Chiba through parts of modern {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|иҢЁеҹҺзңҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Prefecture|qq=}}, {{ill|Saitama Prefecture|ja|еҹјзҺүзңҢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saitama Prefecture|qq=}}, and {{ill|Tokyo|ja|жқұдә¬йғҪ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo|qq=}}, a domain sometimes called "Greater Unakami Kuni."
In the 6th century, emerging central forces established Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko, dividing Kamitsu and Shimotsu Unakami regions. Further decline followed the rise of the {{ill|Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|иҸҠйә»еӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} along the {{ill|Murata River|ja|жқ‘з”°е·қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murata River|qq=}}. The {{ill|Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|ja|е§үеҙҺеҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} is considered the burial site of the Kamitsu Unakami lineage, featuring notable tumuli such as the 130-meter-long {{ill|Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|е§үеҙҺеӨ©зҘһеұұеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (late 4th century), the 86-meter-long Shakazan Kofun (early 5th century), and the 103-meter-long Anegasaki Twin Mounds (late 5th century). Shrinking mound sizes in subsequent centuries reflect the lineage's declining power.
=== Lineage === According to the "{{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}," {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|еӨ©з©Ӯж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi|qq=}}вҖҷs son, {{ill|Takehiratori no Mikoto|ja|е»әжҜ”иүҜйіҘе‘Ҫ|1=Takehi-Nateru|1=Takehi-Nateru|zh|е»әжҜ”иүҜйіҘе‘Ҫ|1=Takehi-Nateru|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehiratori no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}},<ref>Takehiratori no Mikoto is also called жӯҰеӨ·йіҘе‘Ҫ, еӨ©еӨ·йіҘе‘Ҫ, or еӨ©ж—Ҙз…§е‘Ҫ in other sources.</ref> was the progenitor of Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ. The "{{ill|KokuzЕҚ Hongi|ja|еӣҪйҖ жң¬зҙҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KokuzЕҚ Hongi|qq=}}" states that during {{ill|Seimu|ja|жҲҗеӢҷеӨ©зҡҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seimu|qq=}}вҖҷs reign, Ame-no-Hohi's eighth-generation descendant, {{ill|Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|ja|еҝҚз«ӢеҢ–еӨҡжҜ”е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|qq=}}, was appointed as KokuzЕҚ. His grandson {{ill|Hisatsuki no Atai|ja|д№…йғҪдјҺзӣҙ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisatsuki no Atai|qq=}} later became the Shimotsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ under Emperor {{ill|ЕҢjin|ja|еҝңзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢjin|qq=}}. The Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ lineage, linked to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|еҮәйӣІж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=}}, shared ancestry with other KokuzЕҚ, such as the {{ill|Izumo Province KokuzЕҚ|ja|Izumo Province йҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province KokuzЕҚ|qq=}} and {{ill|Musashi Province KokuzЕҚ|ja|жӯҰи”өеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Province KokuzЕҚ|qq=}}.
The "{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|з¶ҡж—Ҙжң¬зҙҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}}" (Shoku Nihongi) mentions that in 767 ({{ill|Jingo Keiun|ja|зҘһиӯ·жҷҜйӣІ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingo Keiun|qq=}} 1), {{ill|Himuro Tokunaga|ja|жӘңеүҚиҲҺдәәе»әйә»е‘Ӯ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Tokunaga|qq=}} of KaijЕҚ District was granted the surname "Kazusa Sukune" ({{ill|е®ҝзҰ°|ja|е®ҝзҰ°|1=Sukune|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/е®ҝзҰ°|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukune|qq=}}). Another entry in 775 ({{ill|HЕҚki|ja|е®қдәҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/HЕҚki|qq=}} 6) notes that he was appointed to an official post in Satsuma, suggesting that he may have been a descendant of the Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|дёҠз·Ҹж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|qq=}}.
The {{ill|Anesaki Shrine|ja|е§үеҹјзҘһзӨҫ|sv|Anesaki-jinja|sv=Anesaki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Shrine|qq=}} is said to have been established by Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto to enshrine the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} and three protective deities. His grandson Oshikane no Mikoto later added {{ill|Nintoku|ja|д»ҒеҫіеӨ©зҡҮ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nintoku|qq=}} to the enshrined deities. The Anegasaki Kofun Cluster is believed to include burial sites for this KokuzЕҚ lineage, which worshiped Ane Saki Shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|ж°ҸзҘһ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|qq=}}.

[[sn:Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko]] [[en:Shanghai Kamitsu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[sn:Shanghai Kamitsu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:дёҠжө·дёҠеӣҪйҖ ]] [[sn:hanghai Kamitsu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
'''Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko''' (дёҠжө·дёҠеӣҪйҖ , also known as Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ) governed the {{ill|KaijЕҚ District, Kazusa Province|ja|жө·дёҠйғЎ (дёҠз·ҸеӣҪ)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KaijЕҚ District, Kazusa Province|qq=}} in [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]], which corresponds to part of present-day [[:en:Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It is also referred to as Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ.
== Overview == The region surrounding the [[:en:YЕҚrЕҚ River|YЕҚrЕҚ River]] basin features archaeological sites such as the {{ill|GЕҚdo Kofun Cluster|lt=GЕҚdo Kofun Cluster|lt=Kamado Kofun Cluster|ja|зҘһй–ҖеҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamado Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, believed to be early 3rd-century keyhole-shaped tombs ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]),<ref>This interpretation as kofun shifts the beginning of the Kofun period significantly earlier. Others suggest these are late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] mound burials.</ref> and the {{ill|Inaridai No.1 Kofun|ja|зЁІиҚ·еҸ°1еҸ·еҸӨеўі|Inaridai No.1 Kofun=Inaridai No.1 Kofun|lt=Inaritai No.1 Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaridai No.1 Kofun|qq=}}, which yielded an iron sword inscribed with the royal title {{ill|ЕҢkimi|ja|жІ»еӨ©дёӢеӨ§зҺӢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢkimi|qq=}}.<ref>While some scholars attribute the title to the Yamato court, others link it to the chief buried in the nearby {{ill|Anegasaki Twin Mounds|ja|е§үеҙҺдәҢеӯҗеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Twin Mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=}}.</ref> These indicate close ties with the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Court]]. In addition, the "[[:en:Man'yЕҚshЕ«|ManyЕҚshЕ«]]" (Volume 14) includes an Eastern song that begins with "Natsuma pulls at the seacoast of KaijЕҚ Lagoon, the boat lingers on the shore as night deepens," suggesting its importance as a hub for waterway traffic.
The placement of {{ill|Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|жӯҰзӨҫеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} territory between Kamitsu Unakami and {{ill|Shimotsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|дёӢжө·дёҠеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} regions implies that the establishment of KokuzЕҚ governance in the BЕҚsЕҚ Peninsula occurred in stages. By the 5th century, the Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ and Shimotsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ collectively exerted influence from central Chiba through parts of modern [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], and [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], a domain sometimes called "Greater Unakami Kuni."
In the 6th century, emerging central forces established Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko, dividing Kamitsu and Shimotsu Unakami regions. Further decline followed the rise of the {{ill|Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|иҸҠйә»еӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} along the {{ill|Murata River|ja|жқ‘з”°е·қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murata River|qq=}}. The {{ill|Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|ja|е§үеҙҺеҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} is considered the burial site of the Kamitsu Unakami lineage, featuring notable tumuli such as the 130-meter-long {{ill|Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|е§үеҙҺеӨ©зҘһеұұеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (late 4th century), the 86-meter-long Shakazan Kofun (early 5th century), and the 103-meter-long Anegasaki Twin Mounds (late 5th century). Shrinking mound sizes in subsequent centuries reflect the lineage's declining power.
=== Lineage === According to the "[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]," {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|еӨ©з©Ӯж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi|qq=}}вҖҷs son, {{ill|Takehiratori no Mikoto|ja|е»әжҜ”иүҜйіҘе‘Ҫ|1=Takehi-Nateru|1=Takehi-Nateru|zh|е»әжҜ”иүҜйіҘе‘Ҫ|1=Takehi-Nateru|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehiratori no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}},<ref>Takehiratori no Mikoto is also called жӯҰеӨ·йіҘе‘Ҫ, еӨ©еӨ·йіҘе‘Ҫ, or еӨ©ж—Ҙз…§е‘Ҫ in other sources.</ref> was the progenitor of Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ. The "{{ill|KokuzЕҚ Hongi|ja|еӣҪйҖ жң¬зҙҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KokuzЕҚ Hongi|qq=}}" states that during [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]вҖҷs reign, Ame-no-Hohi's eighth-generation descendant, {{ill|Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|ja|еҝҚз«ӢеҢ–еӨҡжҜ”е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|qq=}}, was appointed as KokuzЕҚ. His grandson {{ill|Hisatsuki no Atai|ja|д№…йғҪдјҺзӣҙ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisatsuki no Atai|qq=}} later became the Shimotsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ under Emperor [[:en:Emperor ЕҢjin|ЕҢjin]]. The Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ lineage, linked to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|еҮәйӣІж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=}}, shared ancestry with other KokuzЕҚ, such as the {{ill|Izumo Province KokuzЕҚ|ja|Izumo Province йҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province KokuzЕҚ|qq=}} and {{ill|Musashi Province KokuzЕҚ|ja|жӯҰи”өеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Province KokuzЕҚ|qq=}}.
The "[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]" (Shoku Nihongi) mentions that in 767 ([[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingo Keiun]] 1), {{ill|Himuro Tokunaga|ja|жӘңеүҚиҲҺдәәе»әйә»е‘Ӯ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Tokunaga|qq=}} of KaijЕҚ District was granted the surname "Kazusa Sukune" ([[:en:Sukune|Sukune]]). Another entry in 775 ([[:en:HЕҚki|HЕҚki]] 6) notes that he was appointed to an official post in Satsuma, suggesting that he may have been a descendant of the Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|дёҠз·Ҹж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|qq=}}.
The {{ill|Anesaki Shrine|ja|е§үеҹјзҘһзӨҫ|sv|Anesaki-jinja|sv=Anesaki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Shrine|qq=}} is said to have been established by Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto to enshrine the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} and three protective deities. His grandson Oshikane no Mikoto later added [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Nintoku]] to the enshrined deities. The Anegasaki Kofun Cluster is believed to include burial sites for this KokuzЕҚ lineage, which worshiped Ane Saki Shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]].
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{ill|Historical Dictionary of National History|ja|еңӢеҸІеӨ§иҫӯе…ё|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historical Dictionary of National History|qq=}}, Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan. * Sakamoto TarЕҚ & Hirano Kunio, ''Dictionary of Japanese Clan Names,'' Yoshikawa KЕҚbunkan. * ''Encyclopedia of Japanese History,'' Yamakawa Publishing. * ''Great Dictionary of Shinto,'' Rinsen Shoten. * HЕҚga Toshio, ''The Dawn of Koshin and Izumo,'' HЕҚrei Publishing, 2008.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of KokuzЕҚ|ja|еӣҪйҖ гҒ®дёҖиҰ§|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of KokuzЕҚ|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/kamitu.html Walking through the Ancient Tumuli of Kamitsu Unakami Kuni]

Others suggest these are late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] mound burials.</ref> and the {{ill|Inaridai No.1 Kofun|ja|зЁІиҚ·еҸ°1еҸ·еҸӨеўі|Inaridai No.1 Kofun=Inaridai No.1 Kofun|lt=Inaritai No.1 Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaridai No.1 Kofun|qq=}}, which yielded an iron sword inscribed with the royal title {{ill|ЕҢkimi|ja|жІ»еӨ©дёӢеӨ§зҺӢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢkimi|qq=}}.<ref>While some scholars attribute the title to the Yamato court, others link it to the chief buried in the nearby {{ill|Anegasaki Twin Mounds|ja|е§үеҙҺдәҢеӯҗеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Twin Mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=}}.</ref> These indicate close ties with the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Court]]. In the 6th century, emerging central forces established Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko, dividing Kamitsu and Shimotsu Unakami regions. Further decline followed the rise of the {{ill|Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|иҸҠйә»еӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} along the {{ill|Murata River|ja|жқ‘з”°е·қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murata River|qq=}}. The {{ill|Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|ja|е§үеҙҺеҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} is considered the burial site of the Kamitsu Unakami lineage, featuring notable tumuli such as the 130-meter-long {{ill|Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|е§үеҙҺеӨ©зҘһеұұеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (late 4th century), the 86-meter-long Shakazan Kofun (early 5th century), and the 103-meter-long Anegasaki Twin Mounds (late 5th century). The "{{ill|KokuzЕҚ Hongi|ja|еӣҪйҖ жң¬зҙҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KokuzЕҚ Hongi|qq=}}" states that during [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]вҖҷs reign, Ame-no-Hohi's eighth-generation descendant, {{ill|Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|ja|еҝҚз«ӢеҢ–еӨҡжҜ”е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|qq=}}, was appointed as KokuzЕҚ. His grandson {{ill|Hisatsuki no Atai|ja|д№…йғҪдјҺзӣҙ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisatsuki no Atai|qq=}} later became the Shimotsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ under Emperor [[:en:Emperor ЕҢjin|ЕҢjin]]. The "[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]" (Shoku Nihongi) mentions that in 767 ([[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingo Keiun]] 1), {{ill|Himuro Tokunaga|ja|жӘңеүҚиҲҺдәәе»әйә»е‘Ӯ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Tokunaga|qq=}} of KaijЕҚ District was granted the surname "Kazusa Sukune" ([[:en:Sukune|Sukune]]). The {{ill|Anesaki Shrine|ja|е§үеҹјзҘһзӨҫ|sv|Anesaki-jinja|sv=Anesaki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Shrine|qq=}} is said to have been established by Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto to enshrine the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} and three protective deities. {{afc comment|1=Weirdly read as Shanghai no Kuni no Miyatsuko intuitively[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 14:32, 15 May 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko''' (дёҠжө·дёҠеӣҪйҖ , also known as Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ) governed the {{ill|KaijЕҚ District, Kazusa Province|ja|жө·дёҠйғЎ (дёҠз·ҸеӣҪ)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KaijЕҚ District, Kazusa Province|qq=}} in [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]], which corresponds to part of present-day [[:en:Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It is also referred to as Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ.
== Overview == The region surrounding the [[:en:YЕҚrЕҚ River|YЕҚrЕҚ River]] basin features archaeological sites such as the {{ill|GЕҚdo Kofun Cluster|lt=GЕҚdo Kofun Cluster|lt=Kamado Kofun Cluster|ja|зҘһй–ҖеҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamado Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, believed to be early 3rd-century keyhole-shaped tombs ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]),<ref>This interpretation as kofun shifts the beginning of the Kofun period significantly earlier. Others suggest these are late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] mound burials.</ref> and the {{ill|Inaridai No.1 Kofun|ja|зЁІиҚ·еҸ°1еҸ·еҸӨеўі|Inaridai No.1 Kofun=Inaridai No.1 Kofun|lt=Inaritai No.1 Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaridai No.1 Kofun|qq=}}, which yielded an iron sword inscribed with the royal title {{ill|ЕҢkimi|ja|жІ»еӨ©дёӢеӨ§зҺӢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢkimi|qq=}}.<ref>While some scholars attribute the title to the Yamato court, others link it to the chief buried in the nearby {{ill|Anegasaki Twin Mounds|ja|е§үеҙҺдәҢеӯҗеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Twin Mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=}}.</ref> These indicate close ties with the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Court]]. In addition, the "[[:en:Man'yЕҚshЕ«|ManyЕҚshЕ«]]" (Volume 14) includes an Eastern song that begins with "Natsuma pulls at the seacoast of KaijЕҚ Lagoon, the boat lingers on the shore as night deepens," suggesting its importance as a hub for waterway traffic.
The placement of {{ill|Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|жӯҰзӨҫеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} territory between Kamitsu Unakami and {{ill|Shimotsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|дёӢжө·дёҠеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} regions implies that the establishment of KokuzЕҚ governance in the BЕҚsЕҚ Peninsula occurred in stages. By the 5th century, the Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ and Shimotsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ collectively exerted influence from central Chiba through parts of modern [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], and [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], a domain sometimes called "Greater Unakami Kuni."
In the 6th century, emerging central forces established Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko, dividing Kamitsu and Shimotsu Unakami regions. Further decline followed the rise of the {{ill|Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|иҸҠйә»еӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} along the {{ill|Murata River|ja|жқ‘з”°е·қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murata River|qq=}}. The {{ill|Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|ja|е§үеҙҺеҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} is considered the burial site of the Kamitsu Unakami lineage, featuring notable tumuli such as the 130-meter-long {{ill|Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|е§үеҙҺеӨ©зҘһеұұеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (late 4th century), the 86-meter-long Shakazan Kofun (early 5th century), and the 103-meter-long Anegasaki Twin Mounds (late 5th century). Shrinking mound sizes in subsequent centuries reflect the lineage's declining power.
=== Lineage === According to the "[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]," {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|еӨ©з©Ӯж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi|qq=}}вҖҷs son, {{ill|Takehiratori no Mikoto|ja|е»әжҜ”иүҜйіҘе‘Ҫ|1=Takehi-Nateru|1=Takehi-Nateru|zh|е»әжҜ”иүҜйіҘе‘Ҫ|1=Takehi-Nateru|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehiratori no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}},<ref>Takehiratori no Mikoto is also called жӯҰеӨ·йіҘе‘Ҫ, еӨ©еӨ·йіҘе‘Ҫ, or еӨ©ж—Ҙз…§е‘Ҫ in other sources.</ref> was the progenitor of Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ. The "{{ill|KokuzЕҚ Hongi|ja|еӣҪйҖ жң¬зҙҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KokuzЕҚ Hongi|qq=}}" states that during [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]вҖҷs reign, Ame-no-Hohi's eighth-generation descendant, {{ill|Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|ja|еҝҚз«ӢеҢ–еӨҡжҜ”е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|qq=}}, was appointed as KokuzЕҚ. His grandson {{ill|Hisatsuki no Atai|ja|д№…йғҪдјҺзӣҙ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisatsuki no Atai|qq=}} later became the Shimotsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ under Emperor [[:en:Emperor ЕҢjin|ЕҢjin]]. The Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ lineage, linked to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|еҮәйӣІж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=}}, shared ancestry with other KokuzЕҚ, such as the {{ill|Izumo Province KokuzЕҚ|ja|Izumo Province йҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province KokuzЕҚ|qq=}} and {{ill|Musashi Province KokuzЕҚ|ja|жӯҰи”өеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Province KokuzЕҚ|qq=}}.
The "[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]" (Shoku Nihongi) mentions that in 767 ([[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingo Keiun]] 1), {{ill|Himuro Tokunaga|ja|жӘңеүҚиҲҺдәәе»әйә»е‘Ӯ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Tokunaga|qq=}} of KaijЕҚ District was granted the surname "Kazusa Sukune" ([[:en:Sukune|Sukune]]). Another entry in 775 ([[:en:HЕҚki|HЕҚki]] 6) notes that he was appointed to an official post in Satsuma, suggesting that he may have been a descendant of the Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|дёҠз·Ҹж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|qq=}}.
The {{ill|Anesaki Shrine|ja|е§үеҹјзҘһзӨҫ|sv|Anesaki-jinja|sv=Anesaki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Shrine|qq=}} is said to have been established by Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto to enshrine the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} and three protective deities. His grandson Oshikane no Mikoto later added [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Nintoku]] to the enshrined deities. The Anegasaki Kofun Cluster is believed to include burial sites for this KokuzЕҚ lineage, which worshiped Ane Saki Shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]].

Further decline followed the rise of the {{ill|Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|иҸҠйә»еӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} along the {{ill|Murata River|ja|жқ‘з”°е·қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murata River|qq=}}. The {{ill|Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|ja|е§үеҙҺеҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} is considered the burial site of the Kamitsu Unakami lineage, featuring notable tumuli such as the 130-meter-long {{ill|Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|е§үеҙҺеӨ©зҘһеұұеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (late 4th century), the 86-meter-long Shakazan Kofun (early 5th century), and the 103-meter-long Anegasaki Twin Mounds (late 5th century). Shrinking mound sizes in subsequent centuries reflect the lineage's declining power.
=== Lineage === According to the "[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]," {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|еӨ©з©Ӯж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi|qq=}}вҖҷs son, {{ill|Takehiratori no Mikoto|ja|е»әжҜ”иүҜйіҘе‘Ҫ|1=Takehi-Nateru|1=Takehi-Nateru|zh|е»әжҜ”иүҜйіҘе‘Ҫ|1=Takehi-Nateru|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehiratori no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}},<ref>Takehiratori no Mikoto is also called жӯҰеӨ·йіҘе‘Ҫ, еӨ©еӨ·йіҘе‘Ҫ, or еӨ©ж—Ҙз…§е‘Ҫ in other sources.</ref> was the progenitor of Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ. The "{{ill|KokuzЕҚ Hongi|ja|еӣҪйҖ жң¬зҙҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KokuzЕҚ Hongi|qq=}}" states that during [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]вҖҷs reign, Ame-no-Hohi's eighth-generation descendant, {{ill|Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|ja|еҝҚз«ӢеҢ–еӨҡжҜ”е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|qq=}}, was appointed as KokuzЕҚ. His grandson {{ill|Hisatsuki no Atai|ja|д№…йғҪдјҺзӣҙ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisatsuki no Atai|qq=}} later became the Shimotsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ under Emperor [[:en:Emperor ЕҢjin|ЕҢjin]]. The Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ lineage, linked to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|еҮәйӣІж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=}}, shared ancestry with other KokuzЕҚ, such as the {{ill|Izumo Province KokuzЕҚ|ja|Izumo Province йҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province KokuzЕҚ|qq=}} and {{ill|Musashi Province KokuzЕҚ|ja|жӯҰи”өеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Province KokuzЕҚ|qq=}}.
The "[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]" (Shoku Nihongi) mentions that in 767 ([[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingo Keiun]] 1), {{ill|Himuro Tokunaga|ja|жӘңеүҚиҲҺдәәе»әйә»е‘Ӯ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Tokunaga|qq=}} of KaijЕҚ District was granted the surname "Kazusa Sukune" ([[:en:Sukune|Sukune]]). Another entry in 775 ([[:en:HЕҚki|HЕҚki]] 6) notes that he was appointed to an official post in Satsuma, suggesting that he may have been a descendant of the Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|дёҠз·Ҹж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|qq=}}.
The {{ill|Anesaki Shrine|ja|е§үеҹјзҘһзӨҫ|sv|Anesaki-jinja|sv=Anesaki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Shrine|qq=}} is said to have been established by Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto to enshrine the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} and three protective deities. His grandson Oshikane no Mikoto later added [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Nintoku]] to the enshrined deities. The Anegasaki Kofun Cluster is believed to include burial sites for this KokuzЕҚ lineage, which worshiped Ane Saki Shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]].

{{afc comment|1=Weirdly read as Shanghai no Kuni no Miyatsuko intuitively[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 14:32, 15 May 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko''' (дёҠжө·дёҠеӣҪйҖ , also known as Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ) governed the {{ill|KaijЕҚ District, Kazusa Province|ja|жө·дёҠйғЎ (дёҠз·ҸеӣҪ)|1=Unakami district|2=ja|3=жө·дёҠйғЎ (дёҠз·ҸеӣҪ)|comment=not Shanghai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KaijЕҚ District, Kazusa Province|qq=}} in [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]], which corresponds to part of present-day [[:en:Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It is also referred to as Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ.
== Overview == The region surrounding the [[:en:YЕҚrЕҚ River|YЕҚrЕҚ River]] basin features archaeological sites such as the {{ill|GЕҚdo Kofun Cluster|lt=GЕҚdo Kofun Cluster|lt=Kamado Kofun Cluster|ja|зҘһй–ҖеҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamado Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, believed to be early 3rd-century keyhole-shaped tombs ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]),<ref>This interpretation as kofun shifts the beginning of the Kofun period significantly earlier. Others suggest these are late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] mound burials.</ref> and the {{ill|Inaridai No.1 Kofun|ja|зЁІиҚ·еҸ°1еҸ·еҸӨеўі|Inaridai No.1 Kofun=Inaridai No.1 Kofun|lt=Inaritai No.1 Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaridai No.1 Kofun|qq=}}, which yielded an iron sword inscribed with the royal title {{ill|ЕҢkimi|ja|жІ»еӨ©дёӢеӨ§зҺӢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢkimi|qq=}}.<ref>While some scholars attribute the title to the Yamato court, others link it to the chief buried in the nearby {{ill|Anegasaki Twin Mounds|ja|е§үеҙҺдәҢеӯҗеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Twin Mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=}}.</ref> These indicate close ties with the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Court]]. In addition, the "[[:en:Man'yЕҚshЕ«|ManyЕҚshЕ«]]" (Volume 14) includes an Eastern song that begins with "Natsuma pulls at the seacoast of KaijЕҚ Lagoon, the boat lingers on the shore as night deepens," suggesting its importance as a hub for waterway traffic.
The placement of {{ill|Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|жӯҰзӨҫеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} territory between Kamitsu Unakami and {{ill|Shimotsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|дёӢжө·дёҠеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} regions implies that the establishment of KokuzЕҚ governance in the BЕҚsЕҚ Peninsula occurred in stages. By the 5th century, the Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ and Shimotsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ collectively exerted influence from central Chiba through parts of modern [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], and [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], a domain sometimes called "Greater Unakami Kuni."
In the 6th century, emerging central forces established Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko, dividing Kamitsu and Shimotsu Unakami regions. Further decline followed the rise of the {{ill|Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|иҸҠйә»еӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} along the {{ill|Murata River|ja|жқ‘з”°е·қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murata River|qq=}}. The {{ill|Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|ja|е§үеҙҺеҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} is considered the burial site of the Kamitsu Unakami lineage, featuring notable tumuli such as the 130-meter-long {{ill|Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|е§үеҙҺеӨ©зҘһеұұеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (late 4th century), the 86-meter-long Shakazan Kofun (early 5th century), and the 103-meter-long Anegasaki Twin Mounds (late 5th century). Shrinking mound sizes in subsequent centuries reflect the lineage's declining power.
=== Lineage === According to the "[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]," {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|еӨ©з©Ӯж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi|qq=}}вҖҷs son, {{ill|Takehiratori no Mikoto|ja|е»әжҜ”иүҜйіҘе‘Ҫ|1=Takehi-Nateru|1=Takehi-Nateru|zh|е»әжҜ”иүҜйіҘе‘Ҫ|1=Takehi-Nateru|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehiratori no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}},<ref>Takehiratori no Mikoto is also called жӯҰеӨ·йіҘе‘Ҫ, еӨ©еӨ·йіҘе‘Ҫ, or еӨ©ж—Ҙз…§е‘Ҫ in other sources.</ref> was the progenitor of Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ. The "{{ill|KokuzЕҚ Hongi|ja|еӣҪйҖ жң¬зҙҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KokuzЕҚ Hongi|qq=}}" states that during [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]вҖҷs reign, Ame-no-Hohi's eighth-generation descendant, {{ill|Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|ja|еҝҚз«ӢеҢ–еӨҡжҜ”е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|qq=}}, was appointed as KokuzЕҚ. His grandson {{ill|Hisatsuki no Atai|ja|д№…йғҪдјҺзӣҙ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisatsuki no Atai|qq=}} later became the Shimotsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ under Emperor [[:en:Emperor ЕҢjin|ЕҢjin]]. The Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ lineage, linked to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|еҮәйӣІж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=}}, shared ancestry with other KokuzЕҚ, such as the {{ill|Izumo Province KokuzЕҚ|ja|Izumo Province йҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province KokuzЕҚ|qq=}} and {{ill|Musashi Province KokuzЕҚ|ja|жӯҰи”өеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Province KokuzЕҚ|qq=}}.
The "[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]" (Shoku Nihongi) mentions that in 767 ([[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingo Keiun]] 1), {{ill|Himuro Tokunaga|ja|жӘңеүҚиҲҺдәәе»әйә»е‘Ӯ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Tokunaga|qq=}} of KaijЕҚ District was granted the surname "Kazusa Sukune" ([[:en:Sukune|Sukune]]). Another entry in 775 ([[:en:HЕҚki|HЕҚki]] 6) notes that he was appointed to an official post in Satsuma, suggesting that he may have been a descendant of the Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|дёҠз·Ҹж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|qq=}}.
The {{ill|Anesaki Shrine|ja|е§үеҹјзҘһзӨҫ|sv|Anesaki-jinja|sv=Anesaki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Shrine|qq=}} is said to have been established by Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto to enshrine the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}} and three protective deities. His grandson Oshikane no Mikoto later added [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Nintoku]] to the enshrined deities. The Anegasaki Kofun Cluster is believed to include burial sites for this KokuzЕҚ lineage, which worshiped Ane Saki Shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]].

=== Lineage === According to the "[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]," {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|еӨ©з©Ӯж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮўгғЎгғҺгғӣгғ’}}вҖҷs son, {{ill|Takehiratori no Mikoto|ja|е»әжҜ”иүҜйіҘе‘Ҫ|1=Takehi-Nateru|1=Takehi-Nateru|zh|е»әжҜ”иүҜйіҘе‘Ҫ|1=Takehi-Nateru|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehiratori no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}},<ref>Takehiratori no Mikoto is also called жӯҰеӨ·йіҘе‘Ҫ, еӨ©еӨ·йіҘе‘Ҫ, or еӨ©ж—Ҙз…§е‘Ҫ in other sources.</ref> was the progenitor of Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ. The "{{ill|KokuzЕҚ Hongi|ja|еӣҪйҖ жң¬зҙҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KokuzЕҚ Hongi|qq=}}" states that during [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]вҖҷs reign, Ame-no-Hohi's eighth-generation descendant, {{ill|Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|ja|еҝҚз«ӢеҢ–еӨҡжҜ”е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as KokuzЕҚ. His grandson {{ill|Hisatsuki no Atai|ja|д№…йғҪдјҺзӣҙ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisatsuki no Atai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} later became the Shimotsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ under Emperor [[:en:Emperor ЕҢjin|ЕҢjin]]. The Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ lineage, linked to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|еҮәйӣІж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еҮәйӣІеӣҪйҖ }}, shared ancestry with other KokuzЕҚ, such as the {{ill|Izumo Province KokuzЕҚ|ja|Izumo Province йҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province KokuzЕҚ|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Musashi Province KokuzЕҚ|ja|жӯҰи”өеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Province KokuzЕҚ|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ж— йӮӘеҝ—еӣҪйҖ }}.
The "[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]" (Shoku Nihongi) mentions that in 767 ([[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingo Keiun]] 1), {{ill|Himuro Tokunaga|ja|жӘңеүҚиҲҺдәәе»әйә»е‘Ӯ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Tokunaga|qq=}} of KaijЕҚ District was granted the surname "Kazusa Sukune" ([[:en:Sukune|Sukune]]). Another entry in 775 ([[:en:HЕҚki|HЕҚki]] 6) notes that he was appointed to an official post in Satsuma, suggesting that he may have been a descendant of the Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|дёҠз·Ҹж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|qq=}}.
The {{ill|Anesaki Shrine|ja|е§үеҹјзҘһзӨҫ|sv|Anesaki-jinja|sv=Anesaki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Shrine|qq=}} is said to have been established by Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto to enshrine the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©е…җеұӢе‘Ҫ}} and three protective deities. His grandson Oshikane no Mikoto later added [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Nintoku]] to the enshrined deities. The Anegasaki Kofun Cluster is believed to include burial sites for this KokuzЕҚ lineage, which worshiped Ane Saki Shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]].

[[sn:Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko]]
[[sn:Shanghai Kamitsu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:дёҠжө·дёҠеӣҪйҖ ]] [[sn:hanghai Kamitsu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]]
<!--Shanghai Kamitsu no Kuni no Miyatsuko--> [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

{{afc comment|1=Weirdly read as Shanghai no Kuni no Miyatsuko intuitively[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 14:32, 15 May 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko''' (дёҠжө·дёҠеӣҪйҖ , also known as Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ) governed the {{ill|KaijЕҚ District, Kazusa Province|ja|жө·дёҠйғЎ (дёҠз·ҸеӣҪ)|1=Unakami district|2=ja|3=жө·дёҠйғЎ (дёҠз·ҸеӣҪ)|comment=not Shanghai|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KaijЕҚ District, Kazusa Province|qq=}} in [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]], which corresponds to part of present-day [[:en:Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It is also referred to as Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ.
== Overview == The region surrounding the [[:en:YЕҚrЕҚ River|YЕҚrЕҚ River]] basin features archaeological sites such as the {{ill|GЕҚdo Kofun Cluster|lt=GЕҚdo Kofun Cluster|lt=Kamado Kofun Cluster|ja|зҘһй–ҖеҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamado Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, believed to be early 3rd-century keyhole-shaped tombs ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]),<ref>This interpretation as kofun shifts the beginning of the Kofun period significantly earlier. Others suggest these are late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] mound burials.</ref> and the {{ill|Inaridai No.1 Kofun|ja|зЁІиҚ·еҸ°1еҸ·еҸӨеўі|Inaridai No.1 Kofun=Inaridai No.1 Kofun|lt=Inaritai No.1 Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaridai No.1 Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зЁІиҚ·еҸ°1еҸ·еўі}}, which yielded an iron sword inscribed with the royal title {{ill|ЕҢkimi|ja|жІ»еӨ©дёӢеӨ§зҺӢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢkimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ§зҺӢ (гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©)}}.<ref>While some scholars attribute the title to the Yamato court, others link it to the chief buried in the nearby {{ill|Anegasaki Twin Mounds|ja|е§үеҙҺдәҢеӯҗеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Twin Mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=}}.</ref> These indicate close ties with the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Court]]. In addition, the "[[:en:Man'yЕҚshЕ«|ManyЕҚshЕ«]]" (Volume 14) includes an Eastern song that begins with "Natsuma pulls at the seacoast of KaijЕҚ Lagoon, the boat lingers on the shore as night deepens," suggesting its importance as a hub for waterway traffic.
The placement of {{ill|Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|жӯҰзӨҫеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} territory between Kamitsu Unakami and {{ill|Shimotsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|дёӢжө·дёҠеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} regions implies that the establishment of KokuzЕҚ governance in the BЕҚsЕҚ Peninsula occurred in stages. By the 5th century, the Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ and Shimotsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ collectively exerted influence from central Chiba through parts of modern [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], and [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], a domain sometimes called "Greater Unakami Kuni."
In the 6th century, emerging central forces established Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko, dividing Kamitsu and Shimotsu Unakami regions. Further decline followed the rise of the {{ill|Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|иҸҠйә»еӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=}} along the {{ill|Murata River|ja|жқ‘з”°е·қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murata River|qq=}}. The {{ill|Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|ja|е§үеҙҺеҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|qq=}} is considered the burial site of the Kamitsu Unakami lineage, featuring notable tumuli such as the 130-meter-long {{ill|Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|е§үеҙҺеӨ©зҘһеұұеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (late 4th century), the 86-meter-long Shakazan Kofun (early 5th century), and the 103-meter-long Anegasaki Twin Mounds (late 5th century). Shrinking mound sizes in subsequent centuries reflect the lineage's declining power.
=== Lineage === According to the "[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]," {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|еӨ©з©Ӯж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮўгғЎгғҺгғӣгғ’}}вҖҷs son, {{ill|Takehiratori no Mikoto|ja|е»әжҜ”иүҜйіҘе‘Ҫ|1=Takehi-Nateru|1=Takehi-Nateru|zh|е»әжҜ”иүҜйіҘе‘Ҫ|1=Takehi-Nateru|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehiratori no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}},<ref>Takehiratori no Mikoto is also called жӯҰеӨ·йіҘе‘Ҫ, еӨ©еӨ·йіҘе‘Ҫ, or еӨ©ж—Ҙз…§е‘Ҫ in other sources.</ref> was the progenitor of Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ. The "{{ill|KokuzЕҚ Hongi|ja|еӣҪйҖ жң¬зҙҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KokuzЕҚ Hongi|qq=}}" states that during [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]вҖҷs reign, Ame-no-Hohi's eighth-generation descendant, {{ill|Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|ja|еҝҚз«ӢеҢ–еӨҡжҜ”е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as KokuzЕҚ. His grandson {{ill|Hisatsuki no Atai|ja|д№…йғҪдјҺзӣҙ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisatsuki no Atai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} later became the Shimotsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ under Emperor [[:en:Emperor ЕҢjin|ЕҢjin]]. The Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ lineage, linked to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|еҮәйӣІж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еҮәйӣІеӣҪйҖ }}, shared ancestry with other KokuzЕҚ, such as the {{ill|Izumo Province KokuzЕҚ|ja|Izumo Province йҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province KokuzЕҚ|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Musashi Province KokuzЕҚ|ja|жӯҰи”өеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Province KokuzЕҚ|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ж— йӮӘеҝ—еӣҪйҖ }}.
The "[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]" (Shoku Nihongi) mentions that in 767 ([[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingo Keiun]] 1), {{ill|Himuro Tokunaga|ja|жӘңеүҚиҲҺдәәе»әйә»е‘Ӯ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Tokunaga|qq=}} of KaijЕҚ District was granted the surname "Kazusa Sukune" ([[:en:Sukune|Sukune]]). Another entry in 775 ([[:en:HЕҚki|HЕҚki]] 6) notes that he was appointed to an official post in Satsuma, suggesting that he may have been a descendant of the Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|дёҠз·Ҹж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|qq=}}.
The {{ill|Anesaki Shrine|ja|е§үеҹјзҘһзӨҫ|sv|Anesaki-jinja|sv=Anesaki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Shrine|qq=}} is said to have been established by Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto to enshrine the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©е…җеұӢе‘Ҫ}} and three protective deities. His grandson Oshikane no Mikoto later added [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Nintoku]] to the enshrined deities. The Anegasaki Kofun Cluster is believed to include burial sites for this KokuzЕҚ lineage, which worshiped Ane Saki Shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]].

Shrinking mound sizes in subsequent centuries reflect the lineage's declining power.
=== Lineage === According to the "[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]," {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|еӨ©з©Ӯж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮўгғЎгғҺгғӣгғ’}}вҖҷs son, {{ill|Takehiratori no Mikoto|ja|е»әжҜ”иүҜйіҘе‘Ҫ|1=Takehi-Nateru|1=Takehi-Nateru|zh|е»әжҜ”иүҜйіҘе‘Ҫ|1=Takehi-Nateru|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehiratori no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}},<ref>Takehiratori no Mikoto is also called жӯҰеӨ·йіҘе‘Ҫ, еӨ©еӨ·йіҘе‘Ҫ, or еӨ©ж—Ҙз…§е‘Ҫ in other sources.</ref> was the progenitor of Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ. The "{{ill|KokuzЕҚ Hongi|ja|еӣҪйҖ жң¬зҙҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KokuzЕҚ Hongi|qq=}}" states that during [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]вҖҷs reign, Ame-no-Hohi's eighth-generation descendant, {{ill|Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|ja|еҝҚз«ӢеҢ–еӨҡжҜ”е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as KokuzЕҚ. His grandson {{ill|Hisatsuki no Atai|ja|д№…йғҪдјҺзӣҙ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisatsuki no Atai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} later became the Shimotsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ under Emperor [[:en:Emperor ЕҢjin|ЕҢjin]]. The Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ lineage, linked to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|еҮәйӣІж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еҮәйӣІеӣҪйҖ }}, shared ancestry with other KokuzЕҚ, such as the {{ill|Izumo Province KokuzЕҚ|ja|Izumo Province йҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province KokuzЕҚ|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Musashi Province KokuzЕҚ|ja|жӯҰи”өеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Province KokuzЕҚ|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ж— йӮӘеҝ—еӣҪйҖ }}.
The "[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]" (Shoku Nihongi) mentions that in 767 ([[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingo Keiun]] 1), {{ill|Himuro Tokunaga|ja|жӘңеүҚиҲҺдәәе»әйә»е‘Ӯ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Tokunaga|qq=}} of KaijЕҚ District was granted the surname "Kazusa Sukune" ([[:en:Sukune|Sukune]]). Another entry in 775 ([[:en:HЕҚki|HЕҚki]] 6) notes that he was appointed to an official post in Satsuma, suggesting that he may have been a descendant of the Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|дёҠз·Ҹж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|qq=}}.
The {{ill|Anesaki Shrine|ja|е§үеҹјзҘһзӨҫ|sv|Anesaki-jinja|sv=Anesaki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Shrine|qq=}} is said to have been established by Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto to enshrine the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©е…җеұӢе‘Ҫ}} and three protective deities. His grandson Oshikane no Mikoto later added [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Nintoku]] to the enshrined deities. The Anegasaki Kofun Cluster is believed to include burial sites for this KokuzЕҚ lineage, which worshiped Ane Saki Shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]].

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11359572]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Weirdly read as Shanghai no Kuni no Miyatsuko intuitively[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 14:32, 15 May 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko''' (дёҠжө·дёҠеӣҪйҖ , also known as Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ) governed the {{ill|KaijЕҚ District, Kazusa Province|ja|жө·дёҠйғЎ (дёҠз·ҸеӣҪ)|1=Unakami district|2=ja|3=жө·дёҠйғЎ (дёҠз·ҸеӣҪ)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KaijЕҚ District, Kazusa Province|qq=|WD=Q11558726}} in [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]], which corresponds to part of present-day [[:en:Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It is also referred to as Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ.
== Overview == The region surrounding the [[:en:YЕҚrЕҚ River|YЕҚrЕҚ River]] basin features archaeological sites such as the {{ill|GЕҚdo Kofun Cluster|lt=GЕҚdo Kofun Cluster|lt=Kamado Kofun Cluster|ja|зҘһй–ҖеҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamado Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, believed to be early 3rd-century keyhole-shaped tombs ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]),<ref>This interpretation as kofun shifts the beginning of the Kofun period significantly earlier. Others suggest these are late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] mound burials.</ref> and the {{ill|Inaridai No.1 Kofun|ja|зЁІиҚ·еҸ°1еҸ·еҸӨеўі|Inaridai No.1 Kofun=Inaridai No.1 Kofun|lt=Inaritai No.1 Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaridai No.1 Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зЁІиҚ·еҸ°1еҸ·еўі}}, which yielded an iron sword inscribed with the royal title {{ill|ЕҢkimi|ja|жІ»еӨ©дёӢеӨ§зҺӢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢkimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ§зҺӢ (гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©)|1=ЕҢkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q718995}}.<ref>While some scholars attribute the title to the Yamato court, others link it to the chief buried in the nearby {{ill|Anegasaki Twin Mounds|ja|е§үеҙҺдәҢеӯҗеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Twin Mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=}}.</ref> These indicate close ties with the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Court]]. In addition, the "[[:en:Man'yЕҚshЕ«|ManyЕҚshЕ«]]" (Volume 14) includes an Eastern song that begins with "Natsuma pulls at the seacoast of KaijЕҚ Lagoon, the boat lingers on the shore as night deepens," suggesting its importance as a hub for waterway traffic.
The placement of {{ill|Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|жӯҰзӨҫеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546287}} territory between Kamitsu Unakami and {{ill|Shimotsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|дёӢжө·дёҠеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Ukami kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361123}} regions implies that the establishment of KokuzЕҚ governance in the BЕҚsЕҚ Peninsula occurred in stages. By the 5th century, the Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ and Shimotsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ collectively exerted influence from central Chiba through parts of modern [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], and [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], a domain sometimes called "Greater Unakami Kuni."
In the 6th century, emerging central forces established Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko, dividing Kamitsu and Shimotsu Unakami regions. Further decline followed the rise of the {{ill|Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|иҸҠйә»еӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619936}} along the {{ill|Murata River|ja|жқ‘з”°е·қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murata River|qq=|1=Murata River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523431}}. The {{ill|Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|ja|е§үеҙҺеҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|qq=|1=Anesaki Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17191614}} is considered the burial site of the Kamitsu Unakami lineage, featuring notable tumuli such as the 130-meter-long {{ill|Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|е§үеҙҺеӨ©зҘһеұұеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (late 4th century), the 86-meter-long Shakazan Kofun (early 5th century), and the 103-meter-long Anegasaki Twin Mounds (late 5th century). Shrinking mound sizes in subsequent centuries reflect the lineage's declining power.
=== Lineage === According to the "[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]," {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|еӨ©з©Ӯж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮўгғЎгғҺгғӣгғ’|1=Ame no Hohi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940685}}вҖҷs son, {{ill|Takehiratori no Mikoto|ja|е»әжҜ”иүҜйіҘе‘Ҫ|1=Takehi-Nateru|1=Takehi-Nateru|zh|е»әжҜ”иүҜйіҘе‘Ҫ|1=Takehi-Nateru|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehiratori no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}},<ref>Takehiratori no Mikoto is also called жӯҰеӨ·йіҘе‘Ҫ, еӨ©еӨ·йіҘе‘Ҫ, or еӨ©ж—Ҙз…§е‘Ҫ in other sources.</ref> was the progenitor of Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ. The "{{ill|KokuzЕҚ Hongi|ja|еӣҪйҖ жң¬зҙҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KokuzЕҚ Hongi|qq=|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" states that during [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]вҖҷs reign, Ame-no-Hohi's eighth-generation descendant, {{ill|Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|ja|еҝҚз«ӢеҢ–еӨҡжҜ”е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as KokuzЕҚ. His grandson {{ill|Hisatsuki no Atai|ja|д№…йғҪдјҺзӣҙ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisatsuki no Atai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} later became the Shimotsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ under Emperor [[:en:Emperor ЕҢjin|ЕҢjin]]. The Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ lineage, linked to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|еҮәйӣІж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еҮәйӣІеӣҪйҖ }}, shared ancestry with other KokuzЕҚ, such as the {{ill|Izumo Province KokuzЕҚ|ja|Izumo Province йҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province KokuzЕҚ|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Musashi Province KokuzЕҚ|ja|жӯҰи”өеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Province KokuzЕҚ|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ж— йӮӘеҝ—еӣҪйҖ |1=Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504612}}.
The "[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]" (Shoku Nihongi) mentions that in 767 ([[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingo Keiun]] 1), {{ill|Himuro Tokunaga|ja|жӘңеүҚиҲҺдәәе»әйә»е‘Ӯ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Tokunaga|qq=|1=Himuro Tokunaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q115530428}} of KaijЕҚ District was granted the surname "Kazusa Sukune" ([[:en:Sukune|Sukune]]). Another entry in 775 ([[:en:HЕҚki|HЕҚki]] 6) notes that he was appointed to an official post in Satsuma, suggesting that he may have been a descendant of the Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|дёҠз·Ҹж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|qq=|1=Kazusa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360044}}.
The {{ill|Anesaki Shrine|ja|е§үеҹјзҘһзӨҫ|sv|Anesaki-jinja|sv=Anesaki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Shrine|qq=}} is said to have been established by Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto to enshrine the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©е…җеұӢе‘Ҫ}} and three protective deities. His grandson Oshikane no Mikoto later added [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Nintoku]] to the enshrined deities. The Anegasaki Kofun Cluster is believed to include burial sites for this KokuzЕҚ lineage, which worshiped Ane Saki Shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]].

In the 6th century, emerging central forces established Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko, dividing Kamitsu and Shimotsu Unakami regions. Further decline followed the rise of the {{ill|Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|иҸҠйә»еӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619936}} along the {{ill|Murata River|ja|жқ‘з”°е·қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murata River|qq=|1=Murata River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523431}}. The {{ill|Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|ja|е§үеҙҺеҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|qq=|1=Anesaki Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17191614}} is considered the burial site of the Kamitsu Unakami lineage, featuring notable tumuli such as the 130-meter-long {{ill|Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|е§үеҙҺеӨ©зҘһеұұеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (late 4th century), the 86-meter-long Shakazan Kofun (early 5th century), and the 103-meter-long Anegasaki Twin Mounds (late 5th century). Shrinking mound sizes in subsequent centuries reflect the lineage's declining power.
=== Lineage === According to the "[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]," {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|еӨ©з©Ӯж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮўгғЎгғҺгғӣгғ’|1=Ame no Hohi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940685}}вҖҷs son, {{ill|Takehiratori no Mikoto|ja|е»әжҜ”иүҜйіҘе‘Ҫ|1=Takehi-Nateru|1=Takehi-Nateru|zh|е»әжҜ”иүҜйіҘе‘Ҫ|1=Takehi-Nateru|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehiratori no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}},<ref>Takehiratori no Mikoto is also called жӯҰеӨ·йіҘе‘Ҫ, еӨ©еӨ·йіҘе‘Ҫ, or еӨ©ж—Ҙз…§е‘Ҫ in other sources.</ref> was the progenitor of Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ. The "{{ill|KokuzЕҚ Hongi|ja|еӣҪйҖ жң¬зҙҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KokuzЕҚ Hongi|qq=|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" states that during [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]вҖҷs reign, Ame-no-Hohi's eighth-generation descendant, {{ill|Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|ja|еҝҚз«ӢеҢ–еӨҡжҜ”е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as KokuzЕҚ. His grandson {{ill|Hisatsuki no Atai|ja|д№…йғҪдјҺзӣҙ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisatsuki no Atai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} later became the Shimotsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ under Emperor [[:en:Emperor ЕҢjin|ЕҢjin]]. The Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ lineage, linked to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|еҮәйӣІж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еҮәйӣІеӣҪйҖ }}, shared ancestry with other KokuzЕҚ, such as the {{ill|Izumo Province KokuzЕҚ|ja|Izumo Province йҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province KokuzЕҚ|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Musashi Province KokuzЕҚ|ja|жӯҰи”өеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Province KokuzЕҚ|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ж— йӮӘеҝ—еӣҪйҖ |1=Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504612}}.
The "[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]" (Shoku Nihongi) mentions that in 767 ([[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingo Keiun]] 1), {{ill|Himuro Tokunaga|ja|жӘңеүҚиҲҺдәәе»әйә»е‘Ӯ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Tokunaga|qq=|1=hinokumanotoneri no takemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q115530428}} of KaijЕҚ District was granted the surname "Kazusa Sukune" ([[:en:Sukune|Sukune]]). Another entry in 775 ([[:en:HЕҚki|HЕҚki]] 6) notes that he was appointed to an official post in Satsuma, suggesting that he may have been a descendant of the Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|дёҠз·Ҹж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|qq=|1=Kazusa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360044}}.
The {{ill|Anesaki Shrine|ja|е§үеҹјзҘһзӨҫ|sv|Anesaki-jinja|sv=Anesaki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Shrine|qq=}} is said to have been established by Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto to enshrine the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©е…җеұӢе‘Ҫ}} and three protective deities. His grandson Oshikane no Mikoto later added [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Nintoku]] to the enshrined deities. The Anegasaki Kofun Cluster is believed to include burial sites for this KokuzЕҚ lineage, which worshiped Ane Saki Shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]].

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11359572]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=Weirdly read as Shanghai no Kuni no Miyatsuko intuitively[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 14:32, 15 May 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kamitsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko''' (дёҠжө·дёҠеӣҪйҖ , also known as Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ) governed the {{ill|KaijЕҚ District, Kazusa Province|ja|жө·дёҠйғЎ (дёҠз·ҸеӣҪ)|1=Unakami district|2=ja|3=жө·дёҠйғЎ (дёҠз·ҸеӣҪ)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KaijЕҚ District, Kazusa Province|qq=|WD=Q11558726}} in [[:en:Kazusa Province|Kazusa Province]], which corresponds to part of present-day [[:en:Ichihara, Chiba|Ichihara]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]]. It is also referred to as Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ.
== Overview == The region surrounding the [[:en:YЕҚrЕҚ River|YЕҚrЕҚ River]] basin features archaeological sites such as the {{ill|GЕҚdo Kofun Cluster|lt=GЕҚdo Kofun Cluster|lt=Kamado Kofun Cluster|ja|зҘһй–ҖеҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamado Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, believed to be early 3rd-century keyhole-shaped tombs ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]),<ref>This interpretation as kofun shifts the beginning of the Kofun period significantly earlier. Others suggest these are late [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] mound burials.</ref> and the {{ill|Inaridai No.1 Kofun|ja|зЁІиҚ·еҸ°1еҸ·еҸӨеўі|Inaridai No.1 Kofun=Inaridai No.1 Kofun|lt=Inaritai No.1 Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inaridai No.1 Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to зЁІиҚ·еҸ°1еҸ·еўі}}, which yielded an iron sword inscribed with the royal title {{ill|ЕҢkimi|ja|жІ»еӨ©дёӢеӨ§зҺӢ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/ЕҢkimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ§зҺӢ (гғӨгғһгғҲзҺӢжЁ©)|1=ЕҢkimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q718995}}.<ref>While some scholars attribute the title to the Yamato court, others link it to the chief buried in the nearby {{ill|Anegasaki Twin Mounds|ja|е§үеҙҺдәҢеӯҗеЎҡеҸӨеўі|1=Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Twin Mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Futagozuka Kofun|qq=}}.</ref> These indicate close ties with the [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Court]]. In addition, the "[[:en:Man'yЕҚshЕ«|ManyЕҚshЕ«]]" (Volume 14) includes an Eastern song that begins with "Natsuma pulls at the seacoast of KaijЕҚ Lagoon, the boat lingers on the shore as night deepens," suggesting its importance as a hub for waterway traffic.
The placement of {{ill|Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|жӯҰзӨҫеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Kumano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546287}} territory between Kamitsu Unakami and {{ill|Shimotsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|дёӢжө·дёҠеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsu Unakami no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Ukami kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361123}} regions implies that the establishment of KokuzЕҚ governance in the BЕҚsЕҚ Peninsula occurred in stages. By the 5th century, the Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ and Shimotsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ collectively exerted influence from central Chiba through parts of modern [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]], [[:en:Saitama Prefecture|Saitama Prefecture]], and [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]], a domain sometimes called "Greater Unakami Kuni."
In the 6th century, emerging central forces established Musha no Kuni Miyatsuko, dividing Kamitsu and Shimotsu Unakami regions. Further decline followed the rise of the {{ill|Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|ja|иҸҠйә»еӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|qq=|1=Kikuma no Kuni Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11619936}} along the {{ill|Murata River|ja|жқ‘з”°е·қ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murata River|qq=|1=Murata River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11523431}}. The {{ill|Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|ja|е§үеҙҺеҸӨеўізҫӨ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Kofun Cluster|qq=|1=Anesaki Kofun Cluster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17191614}} is considered the burial site of the Kamitsu Unakami lineage, featuring notable tumuli such as the 130-meter-long {{ill|Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|ja|е§үеҙҺеӨ©зҘһеұұеҸӨеўі|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anegasaki Tenjinyama Kofun|qq=}} (late 4th century), the 86-meter-long Shakazan Kofun (early 5th century), and the 103-meter-long Anegasaki Twin Mounds (late 5th century). Shrinking mound sizes in subsequent centuries reflect the lineage's declining power.
=== Lineage === According to the "[[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]]," {{ill|Ame-no-Hohi|ja|еӨ©з©Ӯж—Ҙе‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Hohi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to гӮўгғЎгғҺгғӣгғ’|1=Ame no Hohi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10940685}}вҖҷs son, {{ill|Takehiratori no Mikoto|ja|е»әжҜ”иүҜйіҘе‘Ҫ|1=Takehi-Nateru|1=Takehi-Nateru|zh|е»әжҜ”иүҜйіҘе‘Ҫ|1=Takehi-Nateru|fr|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehiratori no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}},<ref>Takehiratori no Mikoto is also called жӯҰеӨ·йіҘе‘Ҫ, еӨ©еӨ·йіҘе‘Ҫ, or еӨ©ж—Ҙз…§е‘Ҫ in other sources.</ref> was the progenitor of Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ. The "{{ill|KokuzЕҚ Hongi|ja|еӣҪйҖ жң¬зҙҖ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/KokuzЕҚ Hongi|qq=|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" states that during [[:en:Emperor Seimu|Seimu]]вҖҷs reign, Ame-no-Hohi's eighth-generation descendant, {{ill|Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|ja|еҝҚз«ӢеҢ–еӨҡжҜ”е‘Ҫ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was appointed as KokuzЕҚ. His grandson {{ill|Hisatsuki no Atai|ja|д№…йғҪдјҺзӣҙ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hisatsuki no Atai|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} later became the Shimotsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ under Emperor [[:en:Emperor ЕҢjin|ЕҢjin]]. The Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ lineage, linked to the {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|еҮәйӣІж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еҮәйӣІеӣҪйҖ }}, shared ancestry with other KokuzЕҚ, such as the {{ill|Izumo Province KokuzЕҚ|ja|Izumo Province йҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province KokuzЕҚ|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Musashi Province KokuzЕҚ|ja|жӯҰи”өеӣҪйҖ |qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashi Province KokuzЕҚ|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ж— йӮӘеҝ—еӣҪйҖ |1=Musashi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11504612}}.
The "[[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]" (Shoku Nihongi) mentions that in 767 ([[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingo Keiun]] 1), {{ill|Himuro Tokunaga|ja|жӘңеүҚиҲҺдәәе»әйә»е‘Ӯ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Himuro Tokunaga|qq=|1=hinokumanotoneri no takemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q115530428}} of KaijЕҚ District was granted the surname "Kazusa Sukune" ([[:en:Sukune|Sukune]]). Another entry in 775 ([[:en:HЕҚki|HЕҚki]] 6) notes that he was appointed to an official post in Satsuma, suggesting that he may have been a descendant of the Kamitsu Unakami KokuzЕҚ and an ancestor of the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|дёҠз·Ҹж°Ҹ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|qq=|1=Kazusa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360044}}.
The {{ill|Anesaki Shrine|ja|е§үеҹјзҘһзӨҫ|sv|Anesaki-jinja|sv=Anesaki|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Anesaki Shrine|qq=}} is said to have been established by Oshitatetakahitome no Mikoto to enshrine the deities {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|еӨ©е…җеұӢж №е‘Ҫ|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to еӨ©е…җеұӢе‘Ҫ}} and three protective deities. His grandson Oshikane no Mikoto later added [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Nintoku]] to the enshrined deities. The Anegasaki Kofun Cluster is believed to include burial sites for this KokuzЕҚ lineage, which worshiped Ane Saki Shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]].
